label: task_profile_python_best_practices_iterative.json
description: Tasks are auto-generated as fixed focus areas (matching the full AutoCoder behavior).

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Architecture / Project Structure
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for shipment readiness.

Current focus area:
Architecture / Project Structure

Area guidance:
Check: (1) module boundaries respect single-responsibility and stable-dependencies principle, (2) circular imports are absent, (3) internal packages expose a clean public surface via __all__ or explicit re-exports, (4) entry points are thin (no business logic in __main__ or top-level scripts), (5) third-party adapters are isolated behind interfaces so they can be swapped.

Key tools for this area:
- pydeps (dependency graph visualization)
- pylint --disable=all --enable=cyclic-import
- importlab

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:
  - Evaluation and Review: analyze only, do not modify code.
  - Implementation: apply either code restructuring or meaningful architecture documentation for this focus area.
- If adjacent issues are found, list them as dependencies but do not widen scope.
- Include at least two irritation/failure scenarios for this area and define expected behavior.
- Evaluate whether required/optional checks are sufficient for this area and list missing or upgrade candidates.
- Organize findings into the standard output sections as follows:
  ## Summary — Focus Area Goal, Priority, key findings overview
  ## Plan — Audit Criteria, Concrete Refactorings / Improvements, Small Python Example
  ## Acceptance Checklist — Best Practices as pass/fail items, Common Mistakes as anti-pattern checks
  ## Risks — Dependencies To Other Areas, Irritations / Failure Injection and Expected Behavior

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Data Model and Database
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for shipment readiness.

Current focus area:
Data Model and Database

Area guidance:
Check: (1) ORM models define __repr__ and have explicit column types with nullability declared, (2) all mutations use explicit transactions that rollback on exception, (3) schema migrations are versioned and reversible (up/down), (4) N+1 query patterns are identified and resolved via eager loading or batching, (5) connection pooling is configured with sensible limits and timeouts.

Key tools for this area:
- alembic (migrations)
- sqlalchemy (ORM)
- pytest-sqlalchemy
- django-extensions (show_urls, shell_plus)

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:
  - Evaluation and Review: analyze only, do not modify code.
  - Implementation: modify code only for this focus area.
- If adjacent issues are found, list them as dependencies but do not widen scope.
- Include at least two irritation/failure scenarios for this area and define expected behavior.

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Code Style and Linting
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for shipment readiness.

Current focus area:
Code Style and Linting

Area guidance:
Check: (1) codebase passes ruff check or flake8 without suppressions (or suppressions are documented with justification), (2) imports are sorted consistently (isort/ruff), (3) line length is within project-defined limit (≤ 99 chars recommended), (4) no unused imports, unused variables, or dead code, (5) docstring style is consistent and enforced by pydocstyle or ruff D-rules.

Key tools for this area:
- ruff (preferred: lint + format + isort in one)
- black (formatter)
- flake8 + isort (alternative)
- pylint
- pydocstyle / interrogate

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:
  - Evaluation and Review: analyze only, do not modify code.
  - Implementation: apply formatting and linting fixes for this focus area.
- If adjacent issues are found, list them as dependencies but do not widen scope.
- Include at least two irritation/failure scenarios for this area and define expected behavior.
- Evaluate whether required/optional checks are sufficient for this area and list missing or upgrade candidates.

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Type Safety and API Contracts
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for shipment readiness.

Current focus area:
Type Safety and API Contracts

Area guidance:
Check: (1) all public functions and methods have complete parameter and return type annotations, (2) mypy --strict passes or all suppressions are documented, (3) TypedDict / dataclass / pydantic used for structured data instead of plain dicts, (4) Optional and Union types are narrowed before use (no implicit None dereference), (5) Protocol used for structural typing at module boundaries so internal types are not leaked.

Key tools for this area:
- mypy (--strict mode)
- pyright
- beartype (runtime type checking)
- pydantic v2

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Error Handling
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for shipment readiness.

Current focus area:
Error Handling

Area guidance:
Check: (1) no bare except: or except Exception: pass silently swallowing errors, (2) all re-raises use raise X from Y to preserve the exception chain, (3) cleanup code lives in finally blocks or context managers (not in except only), (4) a custom exception hierarchy exists with at least a FatalError / RecoverableError distinction, (5) the top-level handler logs the full traceback before exit and produces a non-zero exit code.

Key tools for this area:
- pylint W0703 (broad-except)
- bandit B110 (try-except-pass)
- flake8-bugbear B001

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:
  - Evaluation and Review: analyze only, do not modify code.
  - Implementation: modify code only for this focus area.
- If adjacent issues are found, list them as dependencies but do not widen scope.

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Logging and Diagnostics
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for shipment readiness.

Current focus area:
Logging and Diagnostics

Area guidance:
Check: (1) logging.getLogger(__name__) used in every module (no print() in production paths), (2) log levels used appropriately (DEBUG for trace, INFO for milestones, WARNING for degraded state, ERROR for failures, CRITICAL for fatal), (3) structured fields attached via extra={} or structlog bind() for machine-parseable context, (4) no secrets, passwords, or PII in log messages or exception messages, (5) log rotation is configured (size or time-based) to prevent unbounded disk growth.

Key tools for this area:
- structlog
- python-json-logger
- logging.handlers.RotatingFileHandler

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: File I/O and Atomic Writes
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for shipment readiness.

Current focus area:
File I/O and Atomic Writes

Area guidance:
Check: (1) writes use a write-to-temp-then-rename (os.replace) pattern for atomicity so partial writes cannot produce corrupt files, (2) pathlib.Path used consistently (no raw os.path string manipulation), (3) all file handles opened inside with blocks (never manually closed), (4) encoding always explicit (encoding='utf-8') — never relying on platform default, (5) path separators not hardcoded (no '/' or '\\' literals — use pathlib operators).

Key tools for this area:
- atomicwrites
- pathlib (stdlib)
- pyfakefs (testing)

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Memory Management and Resource Leaks
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for shipment readiness.

Current focus area:
Memory Management and Resource Leaks

Area guidance:
Check: (1) all file/socket/database/lock resources opened inside with blocks or __enter__/__exit__ pairs — no manual .close() calls, (2) __del__ not used for cleanup (use context managers instead — __del__ is not guaranteed to run), (3) large collections not accumulated in module-level or class-level globals without size limits, (4) weakref.WeakValueDictionary or functools.lru_cache(maxsize=N) used for caches with bounded size, (5) long-running loops do not hold references to objects that are no longer needed (de-reference loop variables when done).

Key tools for this area:
- tracemalloc (stdlib)
- objgraph
- memory_profiler
- pympler

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:
  - Evaluation and Review: analyze only, do not modify code.
  - Implementation: modify code only for this focus area.

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Configuration / Settings / Secrets
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for shipment readiness.

Current focus area:
Configuration / Settings / Secrets

Area guidance:
Check: (1) no secrets hardcoded in source code or committed config files (.env must be in .gitignore), (2) secrets loaded from environment variables or a secret manager (not from plain files on disk), (3) config schema is typed with defaults and validation at application startup (fail fast on missing required values), (4) no mutable global config state that can change at runtime without explicit reload, (5) config layers are explicit and documented: code defaults < config file < environment variable < CLI argument.

Key tools for this area:
- pydantic-settings
- python-dotenv
- dynaconf
- detect-secrets (git pre-commit)

Execution rules:

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Validation and Business Logic
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for shipment readiness.

Current focus area:
Validation and Business Logic

Area guidance:
Check: (1) all external inputs (API requests, file contents, user input) validated at system boundaries before entering business logic, (2) domain rules expressed as explicit guard clauses (if not valid: raise DomainError) with descriptive error messages — not spread silently throughout the code, (3) validation errors carry field-level context (which field failed and why), (4) pydantic / marshmallow / cerberus used for structured schema validation rather than manual isinstance checks, (5) business invariants are covered by unit tests including boundary and invalid-input cases.

Key tools for this area:
- pydantic v2
- marshmallow
- cerberus
- hypothesis (property-based testing of validators)

Execution rules:

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Security
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for shipment readiness.

Current focus area:
Security

Area guidance:
Check: (1) no eval() / exec() on any user-controlled input, (2) all subprocess calls use list form with shell=False (never shell=True with string interpolation), (3) file path inputs sanitized against directory traversal using Path(...).resolve().is_relative_to(allowed_base), (4) all SQL queries use parameterized placeholders — no string concatenation into SQL, (5) secrets, tokens, and PII not logged, not in tracebacks, and not emitted via environment variables or error responses.

Key tools for this area:
- bandit
- semgrep (python ruleset)
- pip-audit (dependency CVEs)
- safety

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Dependency Management
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for shipment readiness.

Current focus area:
Dependency Management

Area guidance:
Check: (1) requirements.txt or pyproject.toml pins exact versions for all direct dependencies to ensure reproducible builds, (2) dev/test dependencies are separated from runtime dependencies (extras_require or optional-dependencies), (3) no known CVEs in direct or transitive dependencies (pip-audit clean), (4) no unused packages installed (pipdeptree shows no orphans), (5) virtual environment usage is documented and a lockfile (pip-compile or poetry.lock) is committed.

Key tools for this area:
- pip-audit
- pipdeptree
- pip-compile (pip-tools)
- poetry / uv
- safety

- Respect phase boundaries:

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Threading / Background Jobs
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for shipment readiness.

Current focus area:
Threading / Background Jobs

Area guidance:
Check: (1) all shared mutable state accessed from multiple threads is protected by threading.Lock, threading.RLock, or queue.Queue, (2) worker threads have an explicit stop mechanism (threading.Event or cancel flag) and shut down cleanly, (3) unhandled exceptions in threads are caught and propagated to the main thread (via queue or Future), (4) daemon=True threads have documented justification — they must not hold locks or open files at shutdown, (5) thread names are set at creation to make profiler/debugger output readable.

Key tools for this area:
- threading (stdlib)
- concurrent.futures
- queue.Queue
- threading.Event

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:
  - Evaluation and Review: analyze only, do not modify code.

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Async I/O, Cancellation, and Timeouts
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for shipment readiness.

Current focus area:
Async I/O, Cancellation, and Timeouts

Area guidance:
Check: (1) no blocking calls inside async def (no time.sleep, no requests.get, no synchronous file I/O — use asyncio.sleep, httpx, aiofiles instead), (2) all coroutines that can hang have timeout wrappers (asyncio.wait_for with explicit timeout), (3) asyncio.CancelledError is never swallowed in an except Exception block, (4) async with / async for used for all async resources (connections, streams), (5) background tasks created with asyncio.create_task are stored in a set and awaited on shutdown to prevent silent discard.

Key tools for this area:
- asyncio (stdlib)
- anyio
- httpx (async HTTP)
- aiofiles
- pytest-asyncio

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:
  - Evaluation and Review: analyze only, do not modify code.
  - Implementation: modify code only for this focus area.

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Serialization and Deserialization
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
improve Python code quality for shipment readiness.

Current focus area:
Serialization and Deserialization

Area guidance:
Check: (1) pickle not used for untrusted input (use JSON/msgpack instead), (2) all deserialized data is schema-validated immediately on parse (not later on use), (3) unknown fields are explicitly rejected or explicitly ignored — not silently forwarded through the system, (4) persistent serialization formats include a schema version field to support migrations, (5) round-trip tests exist (serialize then deserialize and compare).

Key tools for this area:
- pydantic v2 (model_validate)
- msgspec
- jsonschema
- orjson

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: UI Structure and Responsiveness
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for shipment readiness.

Current focus area:
UI Structure and Responsiveness

Area guidance:
Check: (1) no long-running or blocking work (I/O, computation, sleep) on the main/UI thread — all such work offloaded to QThread / QRunnable / asyncio, (2) business logic not embedded in widget event handlers (handlers should call into a controller or service layer), (3) all UI state updates marshalled back to the main thread via Qt signals/slots — no direct widget manipulation from background threads, (4) loading spinners or progress indicators shown during background work so the UI never appears frozen, (5) UI teardown waits for background workers to finish or explicitly cancels them before exiting.

Key tools for this area:
- PySide6 / PyQt6
- QThread / QRunnable / QThreadPool
- Qt signals/slots mechanism

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Performance
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for shipment readiness.

Current focus area:
Performance

Area guidance:
Check: (1) hot paths avoid repeated attribute lookups (cache as local variables), string concatenation in loops (use join), and unnecessary object allocations, (2) large data sets processed with generators or itertools rather than materializing full lists in memory, (3) I/O-bound work not blocking the event loop (async or thread-pool offload), (4) in-process caches have explicit size limits (lru_cache(maxsize=N)) and TTL to prevent unbounded growth, (5) profiling is done before optimizing — identify actual hotspots with cProfile or py-spy before changing code.

Key tools for this area:
- cProfile / profile (stdlib)
- line_profiler
- memory_profiler
- py-spy (sampling profiler)
- scalene

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Tests
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for shipment readiness.

Current focus area:
Tests

Area guidance:
Check: (1) unit tests cover all domain functions including boundary values, None inputs, and error paths, (2) integration tests cover external system boundaries (DB, filesystem, network) with real or containerized dependencies — not mocks, (3) coverage ≥ 80 % on critical modules (use --cov-fail-under), (4) tests are fully deterministic (no random, no datetime.now() without injection, no dependency on test execution order), (5) test names describe the scenario using the pattern test_<what>_when_<condition>_returns_<expected> or similar readable convention.

Key tools for this area:
- pytest
- pytest-cov
- hypothesis (property-based testing)
- pytest-mock / unittest.mock
- pyfakefs (filesystem)
- pytest-asyncio

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Failure Injection and Recovery
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for shipment readiness.

Current focus area:
Failure Injection and Recovery

Area guidance:
Check: (1) disk-full scenario during write handled gracefully (partial file removed, caller notified), (2) network timeout and transient errors trigger retry with backoff rather than immediate failure or silent hang, (3) partial write followed by process restart produces a consistent and recoverable state (no half-written files left as valid), (4) corrupted or missing config on startup shows a clear human-readable error rather than a cryptic traceback, (5) downstream service unavailability degrades gracefully (cached data, reduced functionality, clear user message) rather than cascading crash.

Key tools for this area:
- pytest + unittest.mock
- pyfakefs (disk fault simulation)
- pytest-timeout
- tenacity (retry)

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:
  - Evaluation and Review: analyze only, do not modify code.
  - Implementation: apply either code changes or test additions for this focus area.
- If adjacent issues are found, list them as dependencies but do not widen scope.
- Include at least two irritation/failure scenarios for this area and define expected behavior.
- Evaluate whether required/optional checks are sufficient for this area and list missing or upgrade candidates.
- Organize findings into the standard output sections as follows:
  ## Summary — Focus Area Goal, Priority, key findings overview
  ## Plan — Audit Criteria, Concrete Refactorings / Improvements, Small Python Example
  ## Acceptance Checklist — Best Practices as pass/fail items, Common Mistakes as anti-pattern checks
  ## Risks — Dependencies To Other Areas, Irritations / Failure Injection and Expected Behavior

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Packaging / Deployment / Updates
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for shipment readiness.

Current focus area:
Packaging / Deployment / Updates

Area guidance:
Check: (1) pyproject.toml defines all required metadata (name, version, description, license, classifiers, entry_points), (2) build is reproducible — fresh pip install . in a clean virtual environment works without extra steps, (3) runtime dependencies minimal and version-bounded (lower and upper bounds), (4) update and migration path documented (CHANGELOG, migration guide for breaking changes), (5) wheels are generated, verified with twine check, and the installed package tested before release.

Key tools for this area:
- build (PEP 517 builder)
- twine
- hatch
- nox / tox (multi-env testing)
- pip-audit (post-package CVE check)

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Maintainability / Clean Code / Documentation
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for shipment readiness.

Current focus area:
Maintainability / Clean Code / Documentation

Area guidance:
Check: (1) functions ≤ 30 lines, classes ≤ 200 lines, cyclomatic complexity ≤ 10 per function (radon cc -nc), (2) all public modules, classes, and functions have docstrings covering purpose, parameters, return value, and raised exceptions, (3) no magic numbers or magic strings — use named constants or enums, (4) README covers setup, usage, architecture overview, and how to run tests, (5) CHANGELOG maintained and updated for each release.

Key tools for this area:
- radon (cyclomatic complexity, maintainability index)
- interrogate (docstring coverage)
- pydocstyle
- pylint R0914 (too-many-locals), R0912 (too-many-branches)

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Observability and Operability
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for shipment readiness.

Current focus area:
Observability and Operability

Area guidance:
Check: (1) health-check endpoint or status CLI command available that verifies all critical dependencies (DB, filesystem, external APIs), (2) startup log emits application version and key config values (without secrets) so operators can confirm correct config at a glance, (3) key operations export metrics: latency percentiles, error rate, throughput (Prometheus counters/histograms or equivalent), (4) runbook documents the three most common failure scenarios with diagnosis steps and remediation, (5) structured log format is machine-parseable (JSON) so log aggregation tools can query fields.

Key tools for this area:
- prometheus_client
- opentelemetry-sdk
- structlog
- python-json-logger

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.

iterations.title: Python Best Practices: Final Synthesis and Prioritized Roadmap
iterations.prompt:
Python Best Practices iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Python code quality for shipment readiness.

Current focus area:
Final Synthesis and Prioritized Roadmap

Area guidance:
Aggregate findings from all previously completed focus areas into a single prioritized action plan. Group items into four tiers: (1) Critical / Blocking — must fix before any release, (2) High / Ship-risk — fix before GA, (3) Medium / Technical Debt — schedule for next sprint, (4) Low / Nice-to-have — backlog. For each item include: specific file and function reference, root cause in one sentence, and estimated effort (S/M/L). If a prior area was skipped or not completed, note it explicitly.

Key tools for this area:
- (no new tools — summarize findings from previous areas)
